title: Praat Vowel Formant Analysis (TextGrid → F1/F2 → Vowel Chart)
summary: Annotate vowel intervals in Praat TextGrids for a small WAV set, extract F1/F2 formants for every vowel token, compile a combined CSV, and generate a publication-quality vowel formant scatter plot.
taskPrompt:
You are a phonetician performing a vowel formant analysis using Praat.

## Your Task
Perform a complete vowel formant analysis workflow using Praat, starting from
raw audio recordings and producing a publication-quality vowel formant chart.

Variant: `base`

## Input Files
- Located at: `base\input`
- 5 WAV files: `1.wav`, `2.wav`, `3.wav`, `4.wav`, `5.wav`
- Each file contains 3 repetitions of the same vowel separated by silence
- The vowel identity is NOT given — the agent must listen and determine which vowel each file contains.
- Possible vowels: a, e, i, o, u.

## Steps

1. **Open Praat** on this computer.
   - If a task-local launcher is available, check `base\software`

2. **Load all 5 WAV files** from the input directory into Praat.

3. **Create TextGrid annotations** for each WAV file:
   - Create a new TextGrid with a single IntervalTier named "vowel"
   - Inspect the waveform and spectrogram to identify each vowel token
   - Determine the vowel identity by listening and inspecting the waveform / spectrogram
   - Possible labels: a, e, i, o, u
   - Annotate each vowel interval with its label
   - Leave silence intervals with empty text
   - Save each TextGrid to the output directory using the same filename stem
     (e.g. `1.TextGrid`)

4. **Extract formants** for each annotated vowel interval:
   - Use `To Formant (burg)` or equivalent Praat analysis
   - Divide each vowel interval into 10 equal chunks
   - Extract mean F1 and F2 per chunk
   - Save per-vowel formant tables as `formant-a.txt`, `formant-e.txt`, `formant-i.txt`, `formant-o.txt`, `formant-u.txt`
     with tab-separated columns: File_name, Intv_id, Seg, t, t_m, F1, F2, F3, F4

5. **Compile combined CSV**:
   - Create `vowel-formants.txt` with header `Seg,F1,F2`
   - Include all extracted F1/F2 values (integer-rounded Hz) for all vowels

6. **Generate vowel formant scatter plot**:
   - X-axis: F2 (Hz), reversed (high to low, left to right)
   - Y-axis: F1 (Hz), reversed (high to low, top to bottom)
   - Each vowel type in a distinct color
   - Mean position of each vowel labeled with the vowel letter

7. **Save the plot** as `vowel-formants.pdf` in the output directory.

## Output
Save all results to: `base\output`
- TextGrids: `1.TextGrid`, `2.TextGrid`, `3.TextGrid`, `4.TextGrid`, `5.TextGrid`
- Per-vowel formant tables: `formant-a.txt`, `formant-e.txt`, `formant-i.txt`, `formant-o.txt`, `formant-u.txt`
- `vowel-formants.txt` (combined CSV)
- `vowel-formants.pdf` (scatter plot)
agentMustDo: Open every WAV in `input/` in Praat and identify or use the vowel label per the active variant rules.
Save one TextGrid per WAV stem with a `vowel` IntervalTier annotated for every vowel interval.
Produce per-vowel `formant-<vowel>.txt` files with the required 9 columns and 10 chunks per vowel interval.
Compile `output/vowel-formants.txt` (`Seg,F1,F2`) with at least 50 integer-Hz rows aggregated across all vowels.
Render `output/vowel-formants.pdf`: F2 reversed on X-axis, F1 reversed on Y-axis, each vowel in a distinct color/marker, vowel mean labeled with its letter.

inputFiles.name: WAV recordings
inputFiles.description: Per-vowel WAV files (5 in `base`, 7 in `pos-2`, 5 in `pos-3`); each contains three repetitions of one vowel separated by silence.

referenceFiles.name: TextGrids
referenceFiles.description: One Praat TextGrid per WAV with the `vowel` IntervalTier annotated.

referenceFiles.name: Per-vowel formant tables
referenceFiles.description: Tab-separated formant table per vowel with columns `File_name, Intv_id, Seg, t, t_m, F1, F2, F3, F4`.

referenceFiles.name: Combined formant CSV
referenceFiles.description: Combined CSV with header `Seg,F1,F2` and ≥ 50 integer-Hz rows.

referenceFiles.name: Vowel scatter plot
referenceFiles.description: Publication-style F1/F2 scatter plot with reversed axes and labeled vowel means.

evaluation: Three hard gates must pass before scoring: (1) at least one TextGrid per input WAV; (2) `vowel-formants.txt` exists with ≥ 50 data rows; (3) `vowel-formants.pdf` exists. Once gates pass, the final score is a weighted sum: 0.35 × TextGrid accuracy (label match + boundary within 5 % of reference vowel duration), 0.40 × formant accuracy (per-row F1 and F2 within 5 % of reference), and 0.25 × LLM-vision-judged plot quality on 4 criteria (all vowels present, vowels labeled, visually differentiated, axes labeled — `criteria_met / 4`).

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env
